channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: S&P 500 futures tick lower as oil rises amid reports that U.S. warship turned back in Hormuz: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite are coming off of a solid week, having notched fresh records on Friday.

item.title: Ryan Cohen tells CNBC GameStop could issue more stock to buy the much larger eBay
item.description: GameStop made a takeover bid for eBay, offering $125 per share in a cash-and-stock deal that values the e-commerce platform at roughly $55.5 billion.

item.title: Warsh's take on Fed independence is met with confusion and some concern
item.description: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh is putting his own spin on the independence of monetary policy. Many former Fed officials aren't sold.

item.title: CNBC's Official WNBA Team Valuations 2026: The league has its first $1 billion team
item.description: The average value of a Women's National Basketball Association team is $460 million, according to CNBC's Official WNBA Team Valuations 2026.

item.title: McDonald's likely to bounce as fast-food chain overhauls menus, marketing, UBS says
item.description: McDonald's shares are down 11% over the past three months, but they should gain ground as the company's moves to increase sales begin to pay off, per UBS.

item.title: AI chipmaker Cerebras targets $3.5 billion raise in IPO
item.description: The share sale could give the company a valuation of up to $24.5 billion, compared with $23 billion as of February.

item.title: Rudy Giuliani, former New York mayor and Trump lawyer, hospitalized in critical condition
item.description: President Donald Trump called Rudy Giuliani a "True Warrior, and the Best Mayor in the History of New York City, BY FAR," in a Truth Social post.

item.title: Musk sought settlement with OpenAI before Oakland trial, filing shows
item.description: Musk is seeking changes to OpenAI's leadership and $150 billion in damages from OpenAI and Microsoft, one of its largest investors.

item.title: 'Godspeed my friend': Inside the final hours of Spirit Airlines
item.description: Spirit Airlines ceased operations overnight after a failed government bailout.

item.title: Pirro reveals new Trump attack evidence; Cole Allen removed from 'suicide precautions'
item.description: Until U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro's comments, prosecutors had not disclosed whose bullet hit the agent.

item.title: Weight loss and hair loss: The growing hair treatment market from GLP-1s
item.description: A common side effect of many GLP-1 drugs is hair loss. It's creating a formerly untapped but growing market for hair treatment products.

item.title: Used EV sales are surging — how their ownership costs compare to gas-powered cars
item.description: With more affordable electric vehicles hitting used-car lots, buyers should be aware of some ownership costs that may differ from those that come with gas cars.

item.title: World Cup prize pool nears $900 million as FIFA boosts payouts. Here’s who gets what
item.description: FIFA's upcoming World Cup is set to be the most lucrative on-record, with a 'record breaking' prize pool on offer, as FIFA shrugs off other controversies.

item.title: Greg Abel earns solid scorecard from Berkshire shareholders after first annual meeting
item.description: The reviews from longtime shareholders and professional investors were broadly positive, even as many acknowledged the notable absence of Warren Buffett.

item.title: Pirro appears to drop plans to appeal criminal investigation of Fed Chair Powell
item.description: The U.S. attorney shifted legal strategies in her investigation of Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell just ahead of a deadline.

item.title: 'Bubble effect': Weight loss drug fueled growth is putting the pharma sector at risk, report finds
item.description: Obesity assets represent about 25% of total forecast sales of the late-stage pipeline, while oncology's share has slipped to 20%, Deloitte found.

item.title: OPEC+ announces 188,000 barrels-per-day output increase in first meeting without UAE
item.description: Concerns around production were amplified further last week with news of the UAE's shock departure.

item.title: Earnings playbook: Palantir and Advanced Micro Devices usher in the next week of reports
item.description: Palantir, Advanced Micro Devices, Walt Disney and McDonald's are among the companies set to report.

item.title: Fewer features, more value: Why some Americans are buying simpler cars
item.description: Cloth seats, manual controls and analog gauges are back as buyers look for cars with sticker prices closer to $30,000.

item.title: Big Tech earnings show how big, smart spending can be rewarded by the market
item.description: It's obvious from this quarter that the bubble talk has been proven wrong.

item.title: Denmark faces data center reckoning as power grid overwhelmed by surging demand
item.description: The data center industry is facing a reckoning as governments around the world consider imposing limits on the growth of the power-hungry facilities.

item.title: From laundromat to millions: Codie Sanchez on buying her first business — and the steps to acquire your own
item.description: Codie Sanchez shares tips on buying a business, plus where to find ones for sale.

item.title: India is burning more coal as extreme heat and the Iran war squeeze energy supplies
item.description: India, the world's third-largest emitter of carbon dioxide, is burning more coal as demand for power rises, most of which is coal-fired.

item.title: Airfare amid Iran war: Buy now or wait out the conflict? Experts weigh the risks
item.description: It could be risky to delay buying plane tickets and hoping fares will drop, according to travel experts.

item.title: The nuclear option: Atomic energy could offer Europe hope, say analysts — but it won't be easy
item.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, has exposed which nations are most exposed to disrupted energy imports.

item.title: Healthcare cost surge makes parental paid leave benefits a target for workplace cuts
item.description: As healthcare costs soar and companies search for savings, generous paid parental leave is a benefit being cut, with recent examples from Zoom and Deloitte.

item.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon issued vague credit recession warning, but the bond market has more pressing issues
item.description: As fears about a credit crisis rise, bond investors may be too complacent with Kevin Warsh to become next Fed chair and volatility in rates likely to follow.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shut down. Here's what travelers need to know if they have tickets
item.description: What travelers who are ticketed on Spirit Airlines need to know.

item.title: Israel approves plan to buy F-35 and F-15IA fighter jets from Lockheed, Boeing
item.description: The deal is a first step in a $119 billion plan to bolster Israel's military

item.title: Golden Tempo wins thrilling Kentucky Derby as trainer DeVaux breaks barrier
item.description: Golden Tempo stormed to victory at the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs on Saturday.
